---
title: ""
  html_document:
    css: "W:\\RDA Team\\R\\cc_brandguide.css"
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE
)


#### Setting Up ####

library(usethis)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(htmltools) 
library(htmlwidgets)
library(gtExtras)
library(gt)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)

# lang <- getOption("highcharter.lang")
# lang$thousandsSep <- ","
# options(highcharter.lang = lang)

#Add database connection and source script with functions for pushing to postgres
source("W:\\RDA Team\\R\\credentials_source.R")
options(scipen = 999)

#### Step 1: Pull in data ####
source("./calcs_hateincident.R")
df <- final_df 

# need to update code below to just rename within the chart fx rather than in the df
df <- df %>%
  # clean up labels for visuals
  mutate(race_label=case_when(
    race=="latino" ~ "Latinx",
    race=="aian" ~ "AIAN",
    race=="nh_black" ~ "Black",
    race=="nhpi" ~ "PI",
    race=="swana" ~ "SWANA",
    race=="nh_white" ~ "White",
    race=="nh_asian" ~ 'Asian',
    race=="total" ~ 'Total',
      TRUE~NA))


## COLORS ## source: W:\RDA Team\Communications\Catalyst California Brand Guide_September 2022.pdf
orange <- "#F25922"     # 01 row on data vis colors page & brand colors page
peridot <- "#CEEA01"    # 01 row on data vis colors page & brand colors page
lavender <- "#CEC4E2"   # brand colors page
meteorite <- "#3A207D"  # brand colors page
ccblue <- "#0860BC"     # 00 row on data vis colors page 
ccyellow <-"#FFCC00"    # 01 row on data vis colors page
ccmagenta <- "#733256"  # 01 row on data vis colors page
ccpenny <- "#FF9873"    # 03 row on data vis colors page

black <- "#000000"      # brand colors page
alabaster<-"#FBFBFB"    # brand colors page
gainsboro <- "#DEDEDE"  # brand colors page
rc_white <- "#F7F7F7"   

my_colors <- c(meteorite, ccblue, peridot, orange, ccpenny, lavender, ccyellow, black)

## FONTS ## 





```

## People who have Experienced Hate Incidents


```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
############# STATIC VERSION ############# 

# calculate width based on longest label
max_label_width <- max(nchar(paste0(round(df$rate, 1), "% (", scales::comma(df$raw), ")")))
chart_width <- max(7, max_label_width * 0.15)

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = rate, y = reorder(race_label, rate), fill = race)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(rate, 1), "% (", scales::comma(raw), ")")),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "People Who Have Experienced a Hate Incident\nin the Last 12 Months (%)",
    x = "Experienced Hate Incident (%)",
    y = NULL,
    caption = 'Data Source: California Health Interview Survey 2023-24 Pooled Estimates. Teens and adults were asked "During the\npast 12 months, do you think you directly experienced a hate incident?"\n\nData Notes: AIAN = American Indian / Alaska Native, PI = Pacific Islander, SWANA = Southwest Asian / North African.\nThose three races include people who identify as that race alone or in combination with another race or ethnicity including\nLatinx. Asian, Black and White races include people who identify as that race alone.'
    ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    #text = element_text(family="Inter"),
    plot.title.position = "plot",   # set alignment based on whole image, not chart
    plot.title = element_text(hjust=.5),
    plot.caption.position = "plot", # set alignment based on whole image, not chart
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust=0),  # left align
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 15, l = 0)), # add space btwn x-axis title and caption
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p

ggplot2::ggsave("hate_incident_race.png",
       plot = p,
       width = 7,
       height = 4.5,
       units = "in",
       dpi = 300,
       bg = rc_white)

```
